q: The probability of an event E, denoted as P(E), is always:
sol: The probability of any event lies between 0 (impossible) and 1 (sure) inclusive.

q: The probability of a sure event is:
sol: A sure event is certain to happen, so its probability is 1.

q: Which of the following cannot be the probability of an event?
If P(E) = 0.05, what is the probability of 'not E'?
The sum of the probabilities of all elementary events of an experiment is:
sol: The sum of probabilities of all possible mutually exclusive outcomes is always 1.

q: An event that cannot happen is called:
sol: An event with probability 0 is an impossible event.

q: A bag contains 3 red and 5 black balls. A ball is drawn at random. The probability that it is red is:
sol: P(Red) = Number of red balls / Total balls = 3 / (3 + 5) = 3/8.

q: A die is thrown once. The probability of getting a prime number is:
sol: Prime numbers on a die are 2, 3, 5. So, 3 outcomes out of 6. P = 3/6 = 1/2.

q: The probability of getting a bad egg in a lot of 400 is 0.035. The number of bad eggs in the lot is:
sol: Number of bad eggs = Total eggs × Probability = 400 × 0.035 = 14.

q: In a single throw of two dice, the probability of getting a doublet is:
Two coins are tossed simultaneously. The probability of getting at most one head is:
sol: Outcomes: HH, HT, TH, TT. At most one head means 0 or 1 head: {HT, TH, TT}. P = 3/4.

q: A letter is chosen at random from the English alphabet. The probability that it is a vowel is:
sol: Vowels are A, E, I, O, U (5 letters). Total alphabets = 26. P = 5/26.

q: A card is drawn from a well-shuffled deck of 52 cards. The probability that it is a face card is:
sol: Face cards are J, Q, K of 4 suits = 12 cards. P = 12/52 = 3/13.

q: The probability of getting 53 Sundays in a non-leap year is:
sol: 365 days = 52 weeks + 1 day. 53 Sundays occur if the extra day is Sunday. P = 1/7.

q: If P(E) + P(not E) = x, then the value of x² - 1 is:
sol: P(E) + P(not E) = 1. So, x = 1. Therefore, x² - 1 = 1² - 1 = 0.

q: A box contains cards numbered 6 to 50. A card is drawn at random. The probability that the number is a perfect square is:
sol: Numbers are 6 to 50 (Total = 45). Perfect squares: 9, 16, 25, 36, 49 (5 numbers). P = 5/45 = 1/9 (Simplified), but 5/45 was target or 4/45 check: Total = 50-6+1=45. Squares: 9,16,25,36,49. Count is 5. Option A error check: 5/45 is correct.

q: A child has a die whose six faces show letters: A, B, C, D, E, A. The probability of getting A is:
sol: A appears twice. Total faces = 6. P = 2/6 = 1/3.

q: The probability of drawing a red face card from a deck of 52 cards is:
sol: Red face cards: King, Queen, Jack of Hearts and Diamonds (6 cards). P = 6/52 = 3/26.

q: In a deck of 52 cards, the number of 'Kings of Red color' is:
sol: One King of Hearts and one King of Diamonds.

q: If three coins are tossed, the total number of possible outcomes is:
sol: 2^3 = 8 outcomes (HHH, HHT, HTH, THH, HTT, THT, TTH, TTT).

q: A bag contains 5 red, 8 white and 4 green marbles. The probability that a marble drawn is not white is:
sol: Total = 17. Not white = Red + Green = 5 + 4 = 9. P = 9/17.

q: The probability of getting a number greater than 6 in a single throw of a die is:
sol: A die only has numbers 1-6. Greater than 6 is impossible.

q: A number is selected at random from 1 to 30. The probability that it is a prime number is:
A card is selected from 52 cards. The probability that it is a black Queen is:
sol: Black Queens are Queen of Spades and Queen of Clubs (2 cards). P = 2/52 = 1/26.

q: The probability of an event can be expressed as a percentage, which must be:
sol: Probability as a percent ranges from 0% to 100%.

q: If P(A) = 0.65, then P(not A) is:
In a family of 3 children, the probability of having at least one boy is:
sol: Total outcomes = 8. Only one case has no boys (GGG). P(at least one boy) = 1 - 1/8 = 7/8.

q: The probability of getting an even number on throwing a die is:

The sum of probabilities of an event and its complementary event is:
Which of the following is an elementary event?
o: Getting an even number on a die
Getting a head on tossing a coin
Getting a sum of 7 on two dice
Getting a prime number on a die
sol: An elementary event has only one outcome.

q: If a card is drawn from a deck, what is the probability of getting an Ace?
sol: There are 4 Aces in 52 cards. P = 4/52 = 1/13.

q: A die is thrown once. The probability of getting a number less than 3 is:
In 52 cards, the number of 'Spades' is:
sol: There are 4 suits, each has 13 cards. Spades is one suit.

q: The probability of getting a total of 13 in a single throw of two dice is:
Two coins are tossed. The probability of getting two heads is:
sol: Outcomes: HH, HT, TH, TT. P(HH) = 1/4.

q: A game of chance involves spinning an arrow that stops at numbers 1-8. Probability it stops at an odd number is:

The probability of getting 53 Mondays in a leap year is:
sol: 366 days = 52 weeks + 2 days. 53 Mondays if extra days are (Sun, Mon) or (Mon, Tue). P = 2/7.

q: A box contains 90 discs numbered 1 to 90. The probability of picking a two-digit number is:
sol: Two-digit numbers are 10 to 90. Count = 90 - 10 + 1 = 81. P = 81/90 = 9/10.

sol: Vowels: A, E, A, I (4 vowels). Total letters = 11. P = 4/11.

What is the probability of an impossible event?
sol: By definition, an impossible event has a probability of 0.

q: A bag contains 15 white and some black balls. If P(black) = 3 * P(white), number of black balls is:
sol: Let black balls be x. x/Total = 3 * 15/Total => x = 45.

q: A card is drawn from 52 cards. The probability that it is a King of Hearts is:
sol: There is only one King of Hearts in a deck.

q: If you throw a die, the probability of getting a number divisible by 3 is:
Two dice are thrown. The probability that the sum of numbers is 1 is:
sol: Minimum sum of two dice is 2 (1+1). Sum 1 is impossible.

q: The probability that a non-leap year has 52 Sundays is:
sol: 365 days = 52 weeks + 1 day. 52 Sundays occur if that 1 day is NOT Sunday. P = 6/7.

q: A number x is chosen at random from -3, -2, -1, 0, 1, 2, 3. Probability |x| < 2 is:
sol: x values where |x| < 2 are -1, 0, 1 (3 values). Total = 7. P = 3/7.

q: The probability of getting a multiple of 4 on a die is:
sol: Only 4 is a multiple of 4 on a die. P = 1/6.

sol: Outcomes with 2 tails: TTH, THT, HTT (3 cases). P = 3/8.

sol: Composite numbers on a die: 4, 6. P = 2/6 = 1/3.
